evidenceNeededDraft: Identify the specific electrical_panel or workplace location involved.
Confirm whether physical or administrative safety controls for electrical_contact are in place and operational.
Review training records, inspection checklists, or maintenance documentation relevant to electrical.
Verify conformance with standard citations: 30 CFR 56.12004, 30 CFR 56.12016, 30 CFR 56.12017, 30 CFR 56.12018, 30 CFR 56.12028.
[Advisory Only] Qualified safety personnel must verify specific evidence requirements on-site.
correctiveActionPatternsDraft: Restrict access to or isolate any hazardous conditions related to electrical_contact immediately.
Inspect, repair, or replace any defective components of the affected electrical_panel before returning to service.
Implement engineering, administrative, or PPE controls to minimize risks from electrical_contact.
Instruct workers on safe practices regarding electrical and verify procedural compliance.
Document corrective actions completed and ensure compliance with citations: 30 CFR 56.12004, 30 CFR 56.12016, 30 CFR 56.12017, 30 CFR 56.12018, 30 CFR 56.12028.
[Advisory Only] Corrective action details must be reviewed and approved by qualified safety managers or engineers.
applicabilityDraft: Applies to operations within jurisdiction: msha.
Applies when task, activity, or condition involves electrical_panel or associated subsystems.
Applies when workers are exposed to electrical_contact risks during normal or maintenance operations.
[Advisory Only] Scope of applicability must be validated by a qualified inspector or safety professional.
